name: Crimson Corsairs
description: A sophisticated privateer corporation with a ruthless reputation for legal manipulation and military intimidation.

ink-stains.description: Heavily armored and trained for rapid crew suppression, not prolonged combat.

cartel.reason: Predatory model makes them a primary antagonist to Cartel interests.

independent captains.reason: Practice of hiding piracy and brutality behind a shield of iron-clad, often fabricated, contracts and legal pretexts.

stages.name: The Intercept
stages.description: A massive, red, and heavily armed War Skiff blocks a trade lane or pulls alongside a target vessel, matching its speed.

stages.name: The Hail
stages.description: A formal, officious announcement citing a fabricated violation, such as a breach of contract or navigation violation.

stages.name: The Shakedown
stages.description: The target's captain is presented with a false choice, effectively a contract for their own robbery.

stages.name: The Boarding Action
stages.description: The Ink-Stains initiate a boarding action, using flash-bangs, tear gas, and shotguns to swiftly overwhelm and neutralize any crew resistance.

stages.name: The Severance
stages.description: The fate of a non-compliant captain and crew is grim, with options including destruction, indentured servitude, or abandonment.

pillars.name: Geopolitical Contracting
pillars.description: Serving as deniable corporate mercenaries for major powers, allowing them to monetize fear and legal ambiguity.

pillars.name: The Crimson Shield Racket
pillars.description: A protection racket, offering a 'Crimson Shield Policy' that grants exemption from audits and harassment.

pillars.name: Grey Market Fencing
pillars.description: Efficiently laundering and selling seized goods through black markets operated by the River Folk.

strengths.name: Blood-Resin armor
strengths.description: Exceptionally heavy plating provides immense protection, making them formidable foes.

vulnerabilities.name: Lack of speed
vulnerabilities.description: Their slow War Skiffs make them vulnerable to faster, more agile vessels.

clients.name: Vaneer Concord
clients.description: Regularly contracts the Corsairs to conduct deniable operations against Heartland Alliance grain shipments.

rivals.name: Ghostwind Raiders
rivals.description: Fast, untraceable ships represent a tactical and philosophical threat to the Corsairs.

business partners.name: River Folk
business partners.description: Primary business partners for illicit trade, providing the liquidity necessary to fund the Corsair operation.

strategic implications.name: Economic Disruption
strategic implications.description: Their 'hindrance' tactics can cripple time-sensitive supply chains, directly impacting Cartel profits and partner viability.

strategic implications.name: Network Erosion
strategic implications.description: Predation on smaller, independent captains weakens the logistical network upon which the Cartel relies.

strategic implications.name: Corporate Infiltration
strategic implications.description: The long-term risk of Lord Valerius acquiring legitimate power within the Cartel represents an unacceptable existential threat.
